What is the speed of sound?
The speed of sound varies depending on the medium and its temperature. In dry air at 20 degrees Celsius, it is approximately 343 meters per second (1,125 feet per second).

Tell me about the history of the Eiffel Tower.

The Eiffel Tower was built for the 1889 World's Fair in Paris. It was designed by Gustave Eiffel's company and was intended to be a temporary structure. Initially, many critics opposed it, but it remains an iconic symbol of Paris and a major global landmark.

The boiling point of mercury at standard atmospheric pressure is approximately 356.7 degrees Fahrenheit (100 degrees Celsius).

How deep is the Mariana Trench?
The Mariana Trench is the deepest oceanic trench on Earth. Its deepest point, the Challenger Deep, is approximately 10,935 meters (about 36,000 feet) deep.
